finding.summary: The clean guitar track exhibits a low-frequency-heavy tonal balance relative to its mid and high frequencies, which may result in a muddy or unclear tone.
finding.probable_cause: The track lacks any EQ processing; the recorded signal has a prominent 100 Hz band compared to 1 kHz and 4 kHz bands, suggesting excessive low-end content for a clean guitar part.
finding.confidence_reason: The spectrum data is limited to only three 1/3-octave bands, so a full frequency analysis is not possible; however, the relative levels clearly show a significant imbalance that is unusual for a clean guitar.

evidence_refs.description: 100 Hz band at -18 dBFS, relative to 1 kHz at -24 dBFS (6 dB louder)
4 kHz band at -30 dBFS, which is 6 dB below 1 kHz

proposal.reason: No EQ or other FX are present on the track; the only available adjustments (volume, pan) would not correct the tonal imbalance. A more appropriate move would require adding an EQ plugin, which is not an allowed operation.
